findings.title: Path traversal / archive extraction (zip-slip): untrusted input reaches readFileSync()
findings.message: Cross-file candidate: http input at routes.js:3 may reach the path sink readFileSync() at sink.js:3 through 2 hop(s). Tainted data used as a filesystem path, or an archive extracted without validating entry names (zip-slip). Confine to a base dir (basename/realpath + allow-list) and reject entries that escape it. Heuristic — verify the data actually reaches the sink unsanitized before trusting it.

findings.title: Path traversal / archive extraction (zip-slip): readFileSync() sink (no source path found)
findings.message: Dangerous path sink readFileSync() at safe.js:3 that the cross-file taint pass could NOT connect to an untrusted source (orphan sink). Still worth a look — the source may arrive via a path the summary call-graph misses (framework dispatch, dynamic call, config). Tainted data used as a filesystem path, or an archive extracted without validating entry names (zip-slip). Confine to a base dir (basename/realpath + allow-list) and reject entries that escape it. Confirm whether attacker-controlled data can reach it before trusting it.
